title: World Inflation Rate
summary: Data fetch failed due to connection timeout. No inflation data was retrieved from the World Bank API. The attempted indicator was FP.CPI.TOTL.ZG (Consumer price index) for all countries.
key_points: Attempted to retrieve World Bank global inflation indicator (FP.CPI.TOTL.ZG)
API request failed with HTTPSConnectionPool timeout after 30 seconds
No actual inflation data was received
why_it_matters: Cannot be determined—no data was retrieved. World Bank CPI data is typically used as a macro catalyst for global inflation expectations and central bank policy signals.
macro_context: World Bank inflation data would provide country-level and aggregate CPI figures used for global macro analysis. Since the fetch failed, macro context is unavailable.

issues_found: Source contains only an API error message with no actual data or analysis
No extractable inflation figures, statistics, or research content
Record accurately describes the failure but cannot be archived as meaningful research
event_type 'unknown' is appropriate given no data was retrieved, but source_type 'data_release_cpi' is misleading since no data was released
Source has zero archival value for a finance research archive

notes: This source is not relevant to market structure. The attempted topic (global inflation) is macro-catalyst adjacent, but the source contains no actual data or analysis—only a connection timeout error. The World Bank indicator FP.CPI.TOTL.ZG (Consumer price index) would normally be archiveable as a macro data release, but the failed fetch means there is nothing to archive. Recommend attempting the API call again or finding an alternative source for this data.
